---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size70[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size50[
Explore current international political events in order to illustrate, extend and/or challenge the arguments made by Enloe (2014)
]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class

1. Review cases submitted to Google doc
    - https://docs.google.com/document/d/1Xx88gGxGpt3VZpRe7RDP2DrnrUtA0Y0VDt1RD4Rhlng/edit?usp=sharing

2. You brought a case too!

Patel, Kasha. (2021, Nov 8). How climate change is disproportionately affecting girls in low-income countries. *The Washington Post*.
https://www.washingtonpost.com/weather/2021/11/08/cop26-girls-education-climate-weather/

<br>

This week we've begun exploring international political events using one of the newer critical theories of IR which adopts a feminist lens.

Today I want to hear about the real-world cases you each brought to class and see how they help us think about the usefulness of this approach to explaining international politics.

<br>

Let's start by going around the room to introduce your cases.

Briefly tell us what you picked and either why it interests you or why you think it speaks to the arguments from Monday.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-swirls.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

.size60[**Assignment for Today**]

<br>

.size50[
Bring to class a current international political event that we can use to illustrate the Feminist IR perspective in action.
]

???

### Everybody ready to go with this?

<br>

Let's check all the citations for APA formatting.

### You tell me which entries need to be reformatted.




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-swirls.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

# Introduce Your Case

.size45[
1. What is your event?

2. Why is it "current," "international" and "political"?

3. How does it help us think critically about Feminist approaches to IR?
]

???

Take five minutes and get ready to present your case as an answer to these questions.

Alright, let's hear the cases.

*PRESENT and DISCUSS each*



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/13-1-different_people.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

???

### Based on our cases reviewed today, and the lives you have lived, do we believe that all international political events impact across the genders differently? Why or why not?

<br>

### Do Feminist perspectives help us study power in more useful and complete ways?

<br>

### Does our mainstream media coverage need to change to better acknowledge women's perspectives in their stories?

<br>

### Ultimately, how convinced are you by Enloe (2014) that a feminist lens helps us better explain international politics?
